query: Build an interactive HTML prototype for the new checkout flow with three layout variations
why: Direct request for an HTML artifact with variation axes — the primary job of the frontend-design family

query: Create a slide deck for the Q3 all-hands meeting using our brand colors
why: Slide deck is one of the five named output types; brand color requirement maps to context scouting

query: The reviewer passed the animation prototype, now compound the learnings
why: Compound capture after a PASS verdict is explicitly owned by design-compounder in this family

query: Wireframe the new settings page so we can align on layout before building
why: Wireframe is a named output type in the family contract

query: Test whether the login page is accessible and check contrast ratios
why: QA of an existing page is owned by frontend-qa, not frontend-design; no artifact production is requested

query: Document our design tokens and add them to the style guide
why: Authoring documentation is not artifact production; design-token-spec or a writing workflow handles token documentation

query: Refactor the Button component to use our new token system
why: Component refactoring is a code change in the product codebase, not a design artifact sprint

query: Show me what the new dashboard could look like
why: Exploratory UI mockup request — design-proposer should scope it, confirm output type as ui-mockup, and gate at awaiting_human for approval before proceeding
